reachability_artifacts: built sworn login against a real loopback device-code HTTP fixture followed by sworn account and sworn logout, recording exit codes, redacted output, issuer-bound account.json, and unchanged provider hashes
built sworn account runs for absent, expired, corrupt, conflicting, and unsupported account records

effort_complexity.rationale: See spec.json; behavior is well-defined but spans the real device flow, record lifecycle, output contract, and migration seam.

release_benefit: A SwornAgent user can use account identity without losing provider credentials, changing model routes, purchasing managed inference, or generating unapproved outbound traffic.

positive_scenarios: A user logs in through an approved issuer and sees only authoritative identity and session facts.
A user logs out while provider credentials and notification migration input remain unchanged.
negative_scenarios: An unsafe issuer or redirect cannot receive an existing bearer token.
Missing server tier or expiry cannot be replaced with a locally invented value.
validation.benefit_hypothesis: A truthful identity-only account surface lets hosted-control-plane identity coexist with trustworthy local execution.

design_decisions.choice: Retain authoritative identity/session status with optional server-authored tier and expiry.
options: identity plus optional server-authored tier and expiry
design_decisions.human_decision: Ratified 2026-07-15: retain login status and email, plus tier and expiry only when supplied by the issuer; remove commerce, model, and notification claims.
design_decisions.rationale: The command remains useful without inventing hosted product facts or coupling identity to inference.

design_decisions.choice: Retain SWORN_AUTH_URL only as a scoped new-login issuer selector.
design_decisions.human_decision: Ratified 2026-07-15: scoped login-only issuer selection, canonical issuer binding, no general bearer-host override.
design_decisions.rationale: Self-host and real-binary testing remain possible without redirecting an already-issued credential.
